title: astragalus parishii subsp. perstrictus
intro: Astragalus parishii subsp. perstrictus, also known as the Parish's milkvetch or Parish's astragalus, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its adaptogenic and immune-boosting effects, which make it a popular herb in traditional Chinese medicine. The health benefits of Astragalus parishii subsp. perstrictus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and enhancing the immune system's response to infections. Astragalus parishii subsp. perstrictus can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to promote overall well-being.
